ogTitle: Talbot West vs Talgomine: Climate Safety

heading: Talbot West vs Talgomine — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Talbot West, Western Australia and Talgomine, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Talgomine has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Talbot West (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Talbot West or Talgomine safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Talbot West has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Talgomine scores 52/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Talbot West scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Talgomine scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Talbot West or Talgomine?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Talgomine has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
